old: macOS Apple Silicon (arm64, KleidiAI enabled) build
truth: Stop using the macOS Apple Silicon KleidiAI-enabled build; it has been disabled.
quote: macOS Apple Silicon (arm64, KleidiAI enabled) [DISABLED]

subject: openEuler builds
old: openEuler builds (x86 310p, x86 910b ACL Graph, aarch64 310p, aarch64 910b ACL Graph)
truth: Stop using openEuler builds; they have been disabled.
quote: openEuler x86 (910b, ACL Graph)

from_fact: openeuler builds (x86 310p, x86 910b acl graph, aarch64 310p, aarch64 910b acl graph)

subject: OpenCL backend initialization
old: OpenCL backend init would fail on devices without cl_khr_integer_dot_product
new: OpenCL backend init no longer fails on devices without cl_khr_integer_dot_product
truth: OpenCL backend initialization no longer fails on devices that lack the cl_khr_integer_dot_product extension; dp4a kernels are simply not called on such devices.
quote: opencl: do not fail backend init on devices without cl_khr_integer_dot_product

from_fact: opencl backend init would fail on devices without cl_khr_integer_dot_product
opencl backend init no longer fails on devices without cl_khr_integer_dot_product

subject: openEuler builds
old: openEuler x86 (310p), openEuler x86 (910b, ACL Graph), openEuler aarch64 (310p), openEuler aarch64 (910b, ACL Graph) builds
truth: Stop using openEuler builds; they have been disabled.
quote: openEuler x86 (910b, ACL Graph)

from_fact: openeuler x86 (310p), openeuler x86 (910b, acl graph), openeuler aarch64 (310p), openeuler aarch64 (910b, acl graph) builds

subject: tokenize tool
old: tokenize tool used its own hand-rolled argv parsing, Windows UTF-8 handling and file reading
new: tokenize tool now uses common_params_parse and shared common helpers
truth: The tokenize tool now uses common_params_parse instead of its own custom argument parsing, Windows UTF-8 handling, and file reading.
quote: Migrate the tokenize tool to common_params_parse, replacing its hand-rolled argv parsing, Windows UTF-8 handling and file reading with the shared common helpers.

from_fact: tokenize tool used its own hand-rolled argv parsing, windows utf-8 handling and file reading
tokenize tool now uses common_params_parse and shared common helpers

subject: Error output in TOKENIZE example
truth: Errors in the TOKENIZE example now go through LOG_ERR instead of fprintf(stderr), which may change error message format, destination, or suppression behavior.
quote: Errors now go through LOG_ERR instead of fprintf(stderr).

subject: im2col dst_type for conv ops
old: im2col dst_type varied across different conv ops
new: im2col dst_type is now uniformized across all conv ops
truth: im2col dst_type is now uniformized across all convolution operations, which may change the output type of im2col and break code that relied on the previous per-op type behavior.
quote: ggml: uniformize im2col dst_type for all conv ops (#23660)

from_fact: im2col dst_type varied across different conv ops
im2col dst_type is now uniformized across all conv ops

subject: openEuler builds
truth: Stop using openEuler builds; they have been disabled.
quote:
openEuler:
- [DISABLED]

subject: reasoning tag matching in chat templates
old: force-opened bare <think> templates were matched with trailing whitespace (e.g. <think>\n) causing the bare <think> to be swallowed into content
new: the tag used for prefix split is now trimmed so bare <think> is matched correctly
truth: Reasoning start tags inferred from prior turns are now trimmed of trailing whitespace before matching, so a force-opened bare <think> prefill is matched instead of being swallowed into content.
quote: The reasoning start tag inferred from prior turns can carry trailing whitespace (e.g. <think>\n) while a force-open template prefills a bare <think>. Trim the tag used for the prefix split so the bare prefill is matched instead of being swallowed into content.

from_fact: force-opened bare <think> templates were matched with trailing whitespace (e.g. <think>\n) causing the bare <think> to be swallowed into content
the tag used for prefix split is now trimmed so bare <think> is matched correctly

subject: Nemotron Nano v2 chat behaviour
old: Nemotron Nano v2 chat was broken (regression)
new: Nemotron Nano v2 chat is fixed
truth: The Nemotron Nano v2 chat regression has been fixed, meaning any workarounds or code that relied on the broken behaviour will no longer be needed and may cause issues if kept.
quote: chat : fix Nemotron Nano v2 regression

from_fact: nemotron nano v2 chat was broken (regression)
nemotron nano v2 chat is fixed

subject: Shard numbering
new: Shard number always starts from 00001 onwards
truth: Shard numbering now starts at 00001-of-XXXXX instead of 00000-of-XXXXX.
quote: Shard number always starts from `00001` onwards (e.g. First shard always starts at `00001-of-XXXXX` rather than `00000-of-XXXXX`).

from_fact: shard number always starts from 00001 onwards

subject: Version default
new: Assume v1.0 if version is missing
truth: If a model is missing a version number, assume v1.0 (First Public Release) instead of treating it as unversioned.
quote: If model is missing a version number then assume `v1.0` (First Public Release)

from_fact: assume v1.0 if version is missing

subject: Minimum required naming fields
new: BaseName, SizeLabel, Version are minimum required
truth: All model files must now have at least BaseName, SizeLabel, and Version to be considered valid under the GGUF Naming Convention.
quote: At a minimum all model files should have at least BaseName, SizeLabel, Version, in order to be easily validated as a file that is keeping with the GGUF Naming Convention.

from_fact: basename, sizelabel, version are minimum required

subject: GGUF filename parsing
old: The `parseGGUFFilename` function previously parsed filenames with a different structure (e.g., different field order, different handling of sidecar prefixes, different size label extraction)
new: The `parseGGUFFilename` function now parses filenames according to the new test cases shown, which include sidecar prefixes like 'mtp-' and 'mmproj-', size labels with suffixes like '3.8B-ContextLength4k', and shard suffixes like '00003-of-00009'
truth: The `parseGGUFFilename` function now expects filenames to follow the new format shown in the test cases, with sidecar prefixes, size labels that may include extra info, and shard suffixes.
quote:
const testCases = [
  {filename: 'Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1-KQ2.gguf',                         expected: { Sidecar: null,   BaseName: 'Mixtral',              SizeLabel: '8x7B',     FineTune: null, Version: 'v0.1',   Encoding: 'KQ2',    Type: null, Shard: null}},
  {filename: 'Grok-100B-v1.0-Q4_0-00003-of-00009.gguf',            expected: { Sidecar: null,   BaseName: 'Grok',                 SizeLabel: '100B',     FineTune: null, Version: 'v1.0',   Encoding: 'Q4_0',   Type: null, Shard: "00003-of-00009"}},
  {filename: 'Hermes-2-Pro-Llama-3-8B-v1.0-F16.gguf',              expected: { Sidecar: null,   BaseName: 'Hermes-2-Pro-Llama-3', SizeLabel: '8B',       FineTune: null, Version: 'v1.0',   Encoding: 'F16',    Type: null, Shard: null}},
  {filename: 'Phi-3-mini-3.8B-ContextLength4k-instruct-v1.0.gguf', expected: { Sidecar: null,   BaseName: 'Phi-3-mini',           SizeLabel: '3.8B-ContextLength4k', FineTune: 'instruct', Version: 'v1.0', Encoding: null,    Type: null, Shard: null}},
  {filename: 'mtp-Qwen3-27B-v1.0-Q4_K_M.gguf',                     expected: { Sidecar: 'mtp',  BaseName: 'Qwen3',                SizeLabel: '27B',      FineTune: null, Version: 'v1.0',   Encoding: 'Q4_K_M', Type: null, Shard: null}},
  {filename: 'mmproj-Qwen2-VL-7B-v1.0-F16.gguf',                   expected: { Sidecar: 'mmproj', BaseName: 'Qwen2-VL',           SizeLabel: '7B',       FineTune: null, Version: 'v1.0',   Encoding: 'F16',    Type: null, Shard: null}},
  {filename: 'not-a-known-arrangement.gguf',                       expected: null},
];

from_fact: the `parsegguffilename` function previously parsed filenames with a different structure (e.g., different field order, different handling of sidecar prefixes, different size label extraction)

the `parsegguffilename` function now parses filenames according to the new test cases shown, which include sidecar prefixes like 'mtp-' and 'mmproj-', size labels with suffixes like '3.8b-contextlength4k', and shard suffixes like '00003-of-00009'

subject: GGML_TYPE_Q4_2
truth: Stop using GGML_TYPE_Q4_2; support for this quantization type has been removed entirely.
quote: // GGML_TYPE_Q4_2 = 4, support has been removed

subject: GGML_TYPE_Q4_3
truth: Stop using GGML_TYPE_Q4_3; support for this quantization type has been removed entirely.
quote: // GGML_TYPE_Q4_3 = 5, support has been removed

subject: GGML_TYPE_Q4_0_4_4
truth: Stop using GGML_TYPE_Q4_0_4_4; support for this type has been removed from gguf files.
quote: // GGML_TYPE_Q4_0_4_4 = 31, support has been removed from gguf files

subject: GGUF format version
truth: GGUF files must now use version 3 in the header; version 2 files are no longer valid.
quote: Must be `3` for version described in this spec, which introduces big-endian support.

subject: GGUF metadata key format
new: keys must be valid ASCII, hierarchical lower_snake_case segments separated by '.', max 65535 bytes
truth: Metadata keys must now be valid ASCII, use lower_snake_case segments separated by '.', and be at most 65535 bytes; existing keys violating these rules are invalid.
quote: // - It must be a hierarchical key, where each segment is `lower_snake_case` and separated by a `.`.

from_fact: keys must be valid ascii, hierarchical lower_snake_case segments separated by '.', max 65535 bytes

subject: GGUF tensor name length limit
old: no explicit max length or different limit
new: tensor name must be at most 64 bytes
truth: Tensor names must now be at most 64 bytes; longer names are invalid.
quote: // - It must be at most 2^16-1/65535 bytes long.

from_fact: no explicit max length or different limit
tensor name must be at most 64 bytes

subject: GGUF tensor data offset alignment
old: no alignment requirement or different alignment
new: offset must be a multiple of ALIGNMENT
truth: Tensor data offset must now be a multiple of ALIGNMENT; unaligned offsets are invalid.
quote: Must be a multiple of `ALIGNMENT`. That is, `align_offset(offset) == offset`.

from_fact: no alignment requirement or different alignment
offset must be a multiple of alignment

subject: general.alignment default value
old: If alignment is not specified, assume it is 32
new: If alignment is not specified, assume it is 32
truth: If general.alignment is not specified, readers must assume alignment is 32.
quote: If the alignment is **not** specified, assume it is `32`.

from_fact: if alignment is not specified, assume it is 32

subject: MOSTLY_Q4_2 file type enum value (5)
truth: Stop using MOSTLY_Q4_2 (value 5) in general.file_type; support has been removed.
quote: MOSTLY_Q4_2 = 5 (support removed)

subject: MOSTLY_Q4_3 file type enum value (6)
truth: Stop using MOSTLY_Q4_3 (value 6) in general.file_type; support has been removed.
quote: MOSTLY_Q4_3 = 6 (support removed)

subject: RoPE scaling key
truth: Stop using [llm].rope.scale_linear; use the newer [llm].rope.scaling.* keys instead.
quote: Note that older models may not have these keys, and may instead use the following key: - `[llm].rope.scale_linear: float32`: A linear scale factor for RoPE to adjust the context length. It is recommended that models use the newer keys if possible, as they are more flexible and allow for more complex scaling schemes.

subject: GGUF format v2
truth: In GGUF v2, all countable values (lengths, etc) changed from uint32 to uint64, so code reading uint32 will misread the metadata.
quote: Most countable values (lengths, etc) were changed from `uint32` to `uint64` to allow for larger models to be supported in the future.

subject: GGJT quantization scheme
new: GGJT v3 uses a different quantization scheme
truth: GGJT v3 uses a different quantization scheme that is incompatible with GGJT v1 and v2.
quote: v1, v2 and v3 are identical, but the latter versions use a different quantization scheme that is incompatible with previous versions.

from_fact: ggjt v3 uses a different quantization scheme

subject: GGML format
truth: Stop using the GGML format; it is superseded by GGUF and no longer officially supported.
quote: GGML is primarily used by the examples in `ggml`, while GGJT is used by `llama.cpp` models. Other executors may use any of the three formats, but this is not 'officially' supported.

subject: GGMF format
truth: Stop using the GGMF format; it is superseded by GGUF and no longer officially supported.
quote: GGML is primarily used by the examples in `ggml`, while GGJT is used by `llama.cpp` models. Other executors may use any of the three formats, but this is not 'officially' supported.

subject: GGJT format
truth: Stop using the GGJT format; it is superseded by GGUF and no longer officially supported.
quote: GGML is primarily used by the examples in `ggml`, while GGJT is used by `llama.cpp` models. Other executors may use any of the three formats, but this is not 'officially' supported.

subject: Hugging Face model cache location
old: models downloaded with -hf flag were stored in a custom llama.cpp-specific cache directory
new: models downloaded with -hf are now stored in the standard Hugging Face cache directory
truth: Models downloaded with the -hf flag are now stored in the standard Hugging Face cache directory instead of a llama.cpp-specific location, enabling sharing with other HF tools.
quote: Hugging Face cache migration: models downloaded with `-hf` are now stored in the standard Hugging Face cache directory, enabling sharing with other HF tools.

from_fact: models downloaded with -hf flag were stored in a custom llama.cpp-specific cache directory
models downloaded with -hf are now stored in the standard hugging face cache directory
